name: BeanQuery MCP
what_it_does: BeanQuery MCP server enabling AI agents to query Beancount personal finance ledgers using BeanQuery SQL-like language — running financial queries on plain-text double-entry accounting files, generating custom reports, analyzing spending and income patterns, and integrating Beancount financial data into AI-assisted personal finance analysis.
use_cases: Querying personal expense data by category from budget analysis agents
Generating financial summaries and net worth reports from financial review agents
Analyzing spending trends over time from personal finance agents
Checking account balances and asset allocation from portfolio agents
Finding specific transactions from forensic accounting agents
not_for: Teams not using Beancount for bookkeeping (use Mint, YNAB, or spreadsheet MCPs for other tools)
Business accounting requiring compliance reporting (Beancount is for personal/small business use)
Real-time banking data (Beancount is a ledger for manually entered or imported transactions)
best_when: A Beancount user managing their finances in plain-text ledgers wants AI assistance querying and analyzing their financial data — powerful SQL-like queries make it easy to answer financial questions.
avoid_when: You don't use Beancount or plain-text accounting. For bank-connected finance apps, use those platforms' MCPs.

auth.notes: No authentication — reads local Beancount ledger files. Set BEANCOUNT_FILE environment variable pointing to your main .beancount file. Local access only.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — Beancount is free and open source
pricing.notes: Free tool. Beancount is free open source. MCP server is free open source. Plain-text finance — no external services.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no rate limits; query time depends on ledger size

known_agent_gotchas: BeanQuery SQL syntax is custom — it's not standard SQL; review BeanQuery docs
Beancount ledger must be configured with the file path before use
Financial data is highly personal — handle with care; this is your real financial data
Large ledgers may have slower query times — complex queries on years of data
Community MCP — niche tool for Beancount users; excellent if you're in that community
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local ledger files. No credentials. Financial data is highly sensitive — protect ledger files. No external exposure. All data stays local.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local files. No credentials. Financial data is extremely sensitive — protect file access. No external network. Privacy-first local accounting.
